title: Thottea sumatrana
intro: Thottea sumatrana is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Thottea sumatrana include iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Thottea sumatrana include tea, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory disorders and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Thottea sumatrana is classified within the family Gesneriaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This family is part of the order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes mint, lavender, and snapdragons. The order Lamiales is further grouped into the clade asterids, which includes a diverse range of flowering plants. Asterids are in turn grouped into the larger clade angiosperms, or flowering plants. The classification of Thottea sumatrana reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.

preparations: Thottea sumatrana is used in traditional medicine, and its herbal preparations are made from various parts of the plant, including the leaves and roots. These preparations are often used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. The plant's leaves are typically dried and then made into tea or powder, while the roots are used to make decoctions.
